title: The Epiphany
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver
### January 7, 2019 | "The Epiphany" | Rev. Katie Pearson

---

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This sermon falls on the Feast of the Epiphany (observed on Monday, January 7, 2019, following the Sunday celebration). The primary lectionary texts would be Isaiah 60:1-6, Psalm 72:1-7, 10-14, Ephesians 3:1-12, and Matthew 2:1-12 (the visit of the Magi). The congregation is in the Christmas Season, specifically the Christmastide octave following Epiphany, which emphasizes the revelation of Christ to the Gentiles through the star and the Magi's journey.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the immediate aftermath of General Convention 2018 (held June-July 2018), with implementation of resolutions still underway, particularly regarding sexual misconduct policies that had been significantly strengthened. The Diocese of Colorado was navigating post-convention adjustments to church governance and financial policies. No major bishop elections or significant diocesan controversies were prominent at this specific moment, though the national church remained engaged in ongoing debates about sexuality and inclusion that characterized the post-2018 period.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver experienced typical winter weather patterns in early January 2019 with temperatures in the 20s-30s Fahrenheit and occasional snow. The Colorado state legislature was preparing to convene its 2019 session (beginning January 9), with state budget discussions and several high-profile education bills anticipated. Downtown Denver was focused on holiday cleanup after the December holiday season, with minimal major breaking news in the immediate week before January 7.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The government shutdown that began December 22, 2018, was still ongoing on January 7, 2019, affecting 800,000 federal workers and becoming the longest shutdown in US history. The 2020 presidential campaign was accelerating with early Democratic primary candidates beginning to announce or prepare announcements (Kamala Harris would announce January 16). The stock market had rebounded modestly from its December 2018 losses, though economic uncertainty remained elevated. The Covington Catholic High School incident (January 18 confrontation) was still days away, so the immediate news cycle focused on the shutdown and early 2019 political developments.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Brexit negotiations remained in crisis mode, with Prime Minister Theresa May facing Parliament after the EU withdrawal agreement faced significant opposition and uncertainty about timing remained high. Venezuela's humanitarian crisis was deepening, with Juan Guaidó preparing to challenge Nicolas Maduro's legitimacy (formally occurring January 23). The Yellow Vest protests in France were continuing into their ninth week with ongoing clashes between protesters and police over fuel taxes and economic inequality.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

The cultural moment of early January 2019 was characterized by post-holiday reflection and New Year's resolution culture, with fitness and wellness industries in full promotional swing. The NFL playoffs were beginning (Wild Card games January 12-13), with Denver Broncos fans cautiously optimistic about their team's modest 6-10 record and playoff prospects uncertain. The general American mood was anxious about economic stability, political division (deepened by the shutdown), and an uncertain future, creating a context where themes of light breaking into darkness and revelation of hope would resonate with congregants.
